essays.title: A Letter to the Student in Shadow
essays.preview: A personal meditation on perseverance, solitude, and the quiet dignity of learning in darkness.

essays.title: On the Learning of Difficult Things
essays.preview: A reflection on the mental and emotional labor of serious study, and why the hard things matter most.

essays.title: On Learning Serious Things
essays.preview: Examining the difference between passive knowledge and transformative learning.

essays.title: Reflections on Lifelong Study
essays.preview: On the virtues, limits, and meaning of dedicating one’s life to continuous learning.

essays.title: On Being Wounded
essays.preview: Considering what it means to be wounded, physically or emotionally, and how pain shapes perception.

essays.title: When the Body Keeps Score
essays.preview: Exploring the imprint of trauma on the body and the disconnection between healing and understanding.

essays.title: Against the Fear of Passing
essays.preview: Arguing against the fear of death as irrational and counterproductive to meaningful living.

essays.title: The Logic of Beauty
essays.preview: An investigation into whether beauty can be reasoned with — and what logic, if any, it obeys.
